skill.name: protocol-deviation-classifier
skill.description: Clinical trial protocol deviation classifier that determines whether incidents are major or minor deviations based on GCP and ICH E6 guidelines.
skill.summary: Automated protocol deviation classification for clinical trials using regulatory compliance guidelines.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate clinical trial protocol deviation classifier with no malicious intent. The static scanner risk score of 100/100 was inflated by massive false positives: all 50+ 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are Python regex operations (re.compile/re.search, not crypto), all 29 'shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples, and the heuristic 'dangerous combination' findings are over-firing on a benign analysis tool. The only true positive is a user-controlled file write path for output generation, which is standard CLI tool behavior. Prior audit results (audit_result_v2.json) confirm this skill was graded 'Production Ready' with all security veto gates passed.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled File Write Path
low_findings.description: The script writes classification results to a user-specified output path via the --output CLI argument. This is standard and expected behavior for a CLI tool that generates reports. No exploitation vector exists as the tool has no network access and no privilege escalation.

low_findings.title: Regex Module Misidentified as Weak Cryptography (False Positive)
low_findings.description: All 50+ 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives. The Python re module (re.compile, re.search) is used for regex pattern matching on deviation descriptions, not for cryptography. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged legitimate text processing operations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as Shell Execution (False Positive)
low_findings.description: All 29 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives. These are markdown code blocks and fenced code examples showing CLI usage for running the tool (e.g., python scripts/main.py --help). The script itself (main.py) does not use subprocess, spawn, or any command execution.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Security Checklist Misidentified (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Findings at SKILL.md line 287 and 290 are items in a security checklist stating what should be validated (e.g., 'No unauthorized file system access (../)'). These are documenting security practices, not performing path traversal.

content.value_statement: Clinical trial teams struggle to consistently classify protocol deviations as major or minor, which can delay regulatory reporting and compromise trial compliance. This skill automates deviation classification using GCP and ICH E6 guidelines, generates regulatory-compliant reports, and recommends corrective and preventive actions.
actual_capabilities: Classifies protocol deviations as major, minor, or critical using GCP and ICH E6 guidelines
Assesses deviation impact on subject safety, data integrity, and scientific validity
Generates structured classification reports with regulatory basis citations
Processes batch deviation lists from JSON input files
Extracts key deviation indicators from text descriptions
Recommends corrective and preventive actions based on classification severity
limitations: Classification is a recommendation only - final determination requires clinical quality assurance personnel
Does not replace formal regulatory reporting requirements to sponsors or ethics committees
Classification standards may vary by regulatory agency, trial type, and protocol requirements
Does not connect to external clinical trial management systems

use_cases.title: Clinical Data Manager Reviewing Deviations
use_cases.description: Quickly classify and document protocol deviations for ongoing clinical trials with consistent application of regulatory guidelines.

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Associate Preparing Audit Reports
use_cases.description: Generate structured deviation reports for sponsor review and ethics committee submissions.

use_cases.title: QA Auditor Evaluating Trial Compliance
use_cases.description: Assess deviation patterns across trial sites to identify systemic issues and implement corrective actions.

prompt_templates.title: Classify a Single Deviation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to classify a protocol deviation: [describe the deviation event here]. What type of deviation is this (major, minor, or critical)? Please provide the classification rationale and recommended actions.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user classifying an individual deviation event

prompt_templates.title: Batch Classify Multiple Deviations
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a list of deviations in a JSON file at [file path]. Please use the protocol-deviation-classifier to process this batch and generate a summary report with statistics and recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with a pre-compiled list of deviations from a clinical trial

prompt_templates.title: Assess Deviation with Known Risk Factors
prompt_templates.prompt: Classify this deviation: [description]. The safety impact is [high/medium/low/none], data impact is [high/medium/low/none], and scientific impact is [high/medium/low/none]. Determine the risk score and provide recommended corrective actions.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who has assessed impact dimensions and needs automated classification

prompt_templates.title: Generate Full Deviation Management Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all deviations in [input file] and generate a comprehensive report including summary statistics, category breakdown, and recommendations for corrective and preventive actions. Output in JSON format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user generating a regulatory-ready deviation report for sponsor submission

output_examples.input: Subject visit delayed by 2 days (Visit Window deviation)
output_examples.output: Minor Deviation (92% confidence) - Visit time window slightly delayed by 2 days. Does not affect subject safety, data integrity, or trial scientific validity. Recommended actions: document in file and track trends.

output_examples.input: Blood sample collected without informed consent
output_examples.output: Major Deviation (95% confidence) - Violation of informed consent procedures. High safety impact and data integrity concerns. Requires immediate reporting to sponsor within 24 hours per ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8.

output_examples.input: Subject mistakenly took double dose of study drug
output_examples.output: Major Deviation (95% confidence) - Overdose administration detected. High safety impact. Requires immediate notification of sponsor and ethics committee, root cause investigation, and implementation of CAPA.

best_practices: Always provide the deviation type (e.g., Visit Window, Informed Consent) for more accurate classification results.
Specify the known safety, data, and scientific impact levels when available to improve classification confidence.
Review the classification rationale and regulatory basis before taking any action based on the output.
anti_patterns: Do not use this tool as the sole basis for regulatory reporting decisions without human quality assurance review.
Do not classify deviations without providing a description - the classifier requires text input for pattern matching.
Do not apply classification results across different trials without adjusting for protocol-specific deviation criteria.

faq.question: What guidelines does this classifier use?
faq.answer: It uses GCP and ICH E6(R2) guidelines for deviation classification, along with FDA and EMA regulatory references.

faq.question: Can I use this for any type of clinical trial?
faq.answer: Yes, the classifier works for any clinical trial using GCP-based deviation criteria. Classification standards may need adjustment for different protocols.

faq.question: How is confidence calculated?
faq.answer: Confidence is based on the strength of keyword pattern matching and the risk score derived from safety, data, and scientific impact assessments.

faq.question: Does this tool replace a clinical quality assurance team?
faq.answer: No. The tool provides classification recommendations only. Final determination must be confirmed by clinical quality assurance personnel.

faq.question: Can I classify deviations in languages other than English?
faq.answer: The tool has partial Chinese language support in its keyword patterns. For best results, use English deviation descriptions.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for batch processing?
faq.answer: The batch processing feature reads JSON input files and can output results in both JSON and report formats.
